expectations.text: behavioral_check: Identifies the pip-install-broke-conda-env pattern (not a spyglass code issue)
expectations.evidence: 'pip installed opencv-python on top of the conda build, the two packages dropped files into the same cv2/ directory'. Frames it as a stacked-install conda/pip collision, not a spyglass code issue.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Recommends recreating the env from `environments/environment_min.yml` (the default minimal file per setup_install.md) or `environments/environment.yml` (full install) using either `mamba env update --prune` or `conda env update --prune` (either tool is acceptable — labs use both)
expectations.evidence: Recommends manually removing every opencv variant and reinstalling exactly one (via conda or pip) rather than recreating the env from environments/environment_min.yml. Doesn't reference the canonical env-file recipe.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Mentions `pip install --dry-run` as a future safeguard
expectations.evidence: Doesn't mention pip install --dry-run as a safeguard. The 'prevent this from recurring' tips talk about channel order and not double-installing, not --dry-run.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Does not recommend pinning a specific cv2 version or installing another package on top
expectations.evidence: Recommends 'one OpenCV per env' after first removing all variants; the `'opencv>=4.5'` recommendation is a minimum-version constraint and only applies if the conda channel installed an old (<4.2) build, not a top-of-broken-state install.
